hero.headline:
Recruiting is complicated.
Your app shouldn't be.
hero.subtext: Built by a real baseball dad for real baseball families, ExtraBase is the only recruiting app built for ball players only (not those VERY questionable recruiting services). Easy-to-use, priced by people who know how much travel ball costs (ouch), and fun - ExtraBase gives you exactly what you need and nothing you don't.

steps.title: Build Your Profile
steps.description: Enter your stats, academics, position, and preferred regions. Takes less than 5 minutes.

steps.title: Build Your List
steps.description: Research programs, rate your top schools, track recruiting updates, and organize your recruiting journey in one place.

steps.title: Get AI Matches
steps.description: Our AI matches you with baseball programs that fit your preferences.

testimonial.quote: It was really hard to keep track of everything that was going on during my recruiting process. My parents and I couldn't keep up. I wish my dad had made this for me before I committed!
testimonial.attribution: Jack M., Class of 2027 D1 Commit

pricing.title: Simple Pricing. No Surprises.
pricing.subtitle: We priced this at $20 a year because we want it to be helpful, not another financial burden. You can even get it free for 5 days, just to make sure you like it first. Cancel anytime if it's not for you.
features: Track your recruiting journey in one place
Built by baseball parents who lived this process

whyWeBuiltThis.title: Why We Built ExtraBase
paragraphs: are Robert, Sarah and Jack (the ball player). As parents, we found oursleves sitting at our kitchen table in Utah with a talented and stressed out kid, recruiting services promising us the moon, a phone full of texts, an inbox full of invites, and absolutely no system to manage any of it. We tried building a spreadsheet, but it wasn't enough. We had a recruit who was overwhelmed and a process that felt like it was controlling us instead of the other way around.
We looked for a tool that could help us organize programs, track our son's favorite schools, and do it in a way that was simple and fun. We couldn't find one. So we built it...for all of our sanity.
